response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with impressive breadth across web, mobile, and low-latency systems, but he does not fit this role's mandatory profile. His hands-on C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments over a decade ago plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years on Windows. His database experience is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital, a German investment fintech) is brief and unrelated to fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems. Strong fundamentals cannot substitute for the core stack and domain requirements here.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source tooling authorship
Polyglot adaptability across C/C++, DLang, Java, TypeScript, and Node.js
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, ending in 2015 — nowhere near the required 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital-markets domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; recent stack is MongoDB/MySQL with TypeScript-centric web work

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general systems and low-latency engineering fundamentals
